items.fix: Pin versions you've tested, e.g. openai==1.40.0.
items.why: Unpinned installs can pull a newer, breaking release later.

items.mistake: Committing a raw pip freeze as your only manifest.
items.fix: Maintain a curated direct-dependency list, and generate a full lock separately.
items.why: freeze mixes transitive deps and is hard to maintain by hand.
items.example: Two files: `requirements.in` (3 direct deps, hand-edited) → `pip-compile` → `requirements.txt` (53 pinned including transitives, auto-generated).

items.mistake: Running pip install outside an activated venv.
items.fix: Activate the env (or use its python -m pip) first.
items.why: Otherwise packages install globally and pollute system Python.
items.example:
```bash
which pip            # /usr/bin/pip   ← wrong!
source .venv/bin/activate
which pip            # .venv/bin/pip  ← good
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

items.mistake: Using sudo pip install to bypass permission errors.
items.fix: Install into a virtual environment instead.
items.why: sudo pip can corrupt the system Python managed by the OS.
items.example: Don't: `sudo pip install requests` (writes to `/usr/lib/python3/...`, fights with apt/brew). Do: `python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install requests`.
